chunks.text: In a democratic nation, the constitution functions as the supreme law.
All laws are placed under it
and laws that violate it are treated as false and invalid.
The Japanese Constitution came into force in 1947.
Because it was drafted under foreign occupation
there is criticism that it is " an imposed constitution.
" At the same time
it is also a fact that it has supported the peace and prosperity of postwar Japan.
Amendment requires the support of two - thirds of the Diet and a national referendum.
For this reason, it has never once been amended.
Debate over constitutional revision has continued for many years.
The focus is Article 9, the clause renouncing war.
Supporters argue that amendment is needed to deal with the changing security environment.
Opponents insist that pacifism must be retained.
The results of a poll have swung with the times.
There is a survey suggesting younger generations tend to support revision, but among the elderly
more cautious opinions are common.
The debate over revision requires intellectual honesty.
Rather than " vaguely wanting to change " or " vaguely wanting to preserve," we should discuss specific articles and a specific vision of the nation.
The constitution is not dependent on any one group
it is born and can be changed through the will of the people.
Democracy functions only through the participation of each citizen.
The debate over the constitution is the most fundamental dialogue that decides the future of our nation.
